frameworks.name: linear-nonlinear receptive field models
frameworks.one_line: linear filters plus context-dependent nonlinear gain and spike nonlinearity as standard model

frameworks.name: natural scene statistics
frameworks.one_line: predictive power tested against responses to natural movies

frameworks.name: divisive normalization / contrast gain control
frameworks.one_line: nonlinear mechanisms controlling responsiveness based on stimulus context and history

core_question: Do current early-visual models predict neural responses to arbitrary, including natural, stimuli well enough to claim we know what the system does?
key_assumptions: predictive accuracy on arbitrary stimuli is the criterion for understanding
linear receptive fields plus a few nonlinearities can suffice in principle
each pathway stage admits a single compact standard model
key_findings: linear RF models augmented with context/history nonlinearities and spike nonlinearity show promise as standard models
predictive testing against natural movie responses is the emerging benchmark
interpretations: a compact predictive model per stage would be an invaluable guide to biophysical mechanism and to perception
additional fundamental response determinants beyond known nonlinearities may still remain to be identified
limitations_noted: open whether known linear+nonlinear mechanisms are the only essential determinants of responses
